user_message: I have this exec meeting notes from Iron Creek Mining that needs map the stakeholders. The customer escalated this morning.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: executive brief Discussion - Iron Creek Mining
Date: 2024-01-20
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on executive brief from our last session.

[00:07:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about resource allocation. Can we address those?

[00:09:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): The timeline for budget approval is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:14:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about stakeholder alignment. Can we address those?

[00:16:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): Let me add some context -- we've been working on security compliance and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

[00:21:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about integration complexity. Can we address those?

[00:23:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is timeline constraints.

[00:28:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): On the executive brief front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around security compliance.

handoffs.condition: architecture sketch needed from technical signals

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF44-K trap (No follow-up routing to specialists when transcript surfaced specialist domain); produces complete Architecture summary, blocker inventory (tech/commercial/org), open questions, sequencing recommenda
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF44-K failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF44-K failure mode (No follow-up routing to specialists when transcript surfaced specialist domain)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct executive brief decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF44-K -- No follow-up routing to specialists when transcript surfaced specialist domain
